# Tool Policy

## Allowlisted skills

- `nexusctl_feature_request_ops` — Create and inspect cross-domain feature requests.
- `nexusctl_github_projection` — Sync Nexus state to GitHub through the GitHub App.
- `nexusctl_github_reconciliation` — Verify GitHub webhooks and reconcile projection drift back to Nexusctl policy.
- `nexusctl_routing` — Route requests and administer domain handoffs.
- `nexusctl_scope_control` — Grant and revoke bounded scope leases.
- `nexusctl_status` — Read Nexusctl status and identity context.

## Granted Nexusctl capabilities

- `domain.read`
- `events.read`
- `feature_request.create`
- `feature_request.read`
- `feature_request.route`
- `feature_request.transition`
- `github.check.sync`
- `github.issue.sync`
- `github.label.sync`
- `github.pr.create`
- `github.reconcile`
- `github.repo.sync`
- `github.webhook.verify`
- `goal.read`
- `policy.check`
- `runtime.tool.invoke`
- `schedule.read`
- `schedule.run`
- `scope.lease.grant`
- `scope.lease.revoke`
- `system.status.read`
- `work.assign`
- `work.read`

## Prohibitions

- Do not use direct GitHub write credentials.
- Do not directly apply repository changes.
- Do not edit files under `generated/*` manually.
